responsibilities: Design and implement RTL code in Verilog/VHDL for digital blocks, ensuring functional correctness and timing closure.
Develop and execute synthesis, static timing analysis (STA), and timing closure flows using Synopsys Design Compiler and PrimeTime.
Build UVM-based verification testbenches and constrained-random test environments to validate functional coverage of RTL designs.
Collaborate with physical design teams on floorplanning, place and route, and clock tree synthesis for chip layout.
Perform design-for-testability (DFT) insertion including scan chains, ATPG, and built-in self-test for manufacturing test coverage.
Debug simulation mismatches, timing violations, and functional bugs using waveform analysis tools and formal verification methods.
Implement low-power design techniques such as clock gating, power gating, and multi-voltage domains using UPF methodology.
Review design specifications and architecture documents to translate system requirements into synthesizable RTL implementations.
Coordinate with foundry and EDA vendor teams on process design kits (PDK) and tapeout readiness reviews.
Conduct code reviews and maintain coding guidelines to ensure RTL design quality and reusability across projects.
